message:
🌱 Add kong: Error: stream_api handler "prometheus" response is 26280 mission (#1580)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,76 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "kong-7377-error-stream-api-handler-prometheus-response-is-26280-bytes-only-8000-",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "kong: Error: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280 bytes.  Only 8000 bytes is supported",
+    "description": "Error: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280 bytes.  Only 8000 bytes is supported. This issue affects 5+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify kong troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your kong deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for error: `/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/tools/stream_api.lua:109: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280 bytes.  Only 8`"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review kong configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant kong configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong\nkubectl get configmap -n kong -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kong\n```\n- Kong version (`2.4.1`) using declarative config (db-less) running via docker."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Error: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280…",
+        "description": "This re-implements the stream API with TCP instead of UDP in order to allow for larger response payloads without having to handle chunking/fragmenting things ourselves.\n\nPerformance-wise, keep-alive is used to cut down on per-request connection overhead, and the message payload is sent raw instead\n```yaml\n2021/05/26 19:58:36 [error] 27#0: *112517 lua entry thread aborted: runtime error: /usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/tools/stream_api.lua:109: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280 bytes.  Only 8000 bytes is supported\nstack traceback:\ncoroutine 0:\n\t[C]: in function 'error'\n\t/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/tools/stream_api.lua:109: in function 'handle'\n\t/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/init.lua:1467: in function 'stream_api'\n\tcontent_by_lua(nginx-kong-stream.conf:116):2: in main chunk, udp client: unix:, server: unix:/usr/local/kong/stream_rpc.sock\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Error: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kong -n kong --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n kong --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that `/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/tools/stream_api.lua:109: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280 bytes.  Only 8` no longer appears in logs."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This re-implements the stream API with TCP instead of UDP in order to allow for larger response payloads without having to handle chunking/fragmenting things ourselves.\n\nPerformance-wise, keep-alive is used to cut down on per-request connection overhead, and the message payload is sent raw instead of being packed/unpacked on either side.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "2021/05/26 19:58:36 [error] 27#0: *112517 lua entry thread aborted: runtime error: /usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/tools/stream_api.lua:109: stream_api handler \"prometheus\" response is 26280 bytes.  Only 8000 bytes is supported\nstack traceback:\ncoroutine 0:\n\t[C]: in function 'error'\n\t/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/tools/stream_api.lua:109: in function 'handle'\n\t/usr/local/share/lua/5.1/kong/init.lua:1467: in function 'stream_api'\n\tcontent_by_lua(nginx-kong-stream.conf:116):2: in main chunk, udp client: unix:, server: unix:/usr/local/kong/stream_rpc.sock",
+        "- This started happening after enabling a UDP stream service. With only http services the error does not happen.\n\n### Steps To Reproduce\n\n1. Have multiple HTTP services\n2. Have `prometheus` plugin enabled globally\n3. All is good\n4. Enable a UDP stream service\n5. Observe `kong admin error` logs: every time prometheus server scrapes the kong exporter (in our case 30s) we see the mentioned error.\n\n### Additional Details & Logs\n- Kong node info:"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "kong",
+      "community",
+      "api-gateway",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "kong"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "expert",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/Kong/kong/issues/7377",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/Kong/kong",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/Kong/kong/pull/8641"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 25,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with kong installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-20T06:48:58.023Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
